tasks.boundary: DONE 2026-07-03 (PR #7 merged 332f0d9 + Codex P1 fix 6804b0f in PR #7): Make the CER gap-prompt's website-reply branch robust (Codex P2s on PR #6). When Sophia asks 'what's the company called? (a name, a website, or a deck all work)' and the founder re

Define and wire the dual-jurisdiction founder persona (AU Pty Ltd + SG Pte Ltd) as a distinct proof360 segment. This founder needs cross-jurisdiction program matching — AU grants (R&D Tax, EMDG, NSW MVP) alongside SG grants (BGP, EDG, MRA, Startup SG). CORPUS already has both stacks ingested. Persona should surface automatically when both entity types are detected or self-reported.

Redesign proof360 onboarding flow as Q&A rather than a gap report presentation. Instead of showing the report, ask the founder questions — business model, cloud provider, team size, compliance posture — and let the scored report emerge from their answers. Each confirmation morphs the recommendations in real time.
proof360 signal/scoring methodology convergence remains ready when John returns to it

Wire signal-extractor.js to detect 5 new signals: uses_ai, handles_personal_data, pen_test_completed, has_backup, aws_program_enrolled. Without these, the 5 new gaps (ai_governance, data_privacy, penetration_testing, backup_dr, aws_program_eligibility) cannot fire on live cold-reads.

Before any gap catalog or vendor catalog change ships, explicitly enumerate what is missing and what vendor closes it — do not declare a catalog complete without naming the delta. Codify this as a review step in the gap authoring process.

Surface CORPUS evidence citations in the proof360 frontend evidence display. The gap API response already includes evidence items with source:'corpus' and a 'Retrieved from [source] · CORPUS' citation string — wire this into the gap evidence UI component so users see the retrieval chain. Do not show it if no corpus evidence is present.

Add a catch-all route (path='*') to frontend/src/App.jsx that redirects any unmatched path to /chat, so deleted/legacy paths (/audit, /home, /report, /processing) never render a blank SPA shell. Small HX polish surfaced by the 2026-05-29 legacy strip.

Disposition the 2026-06-10 estate audit findings in one focused pass, bucketed delete/adapter/ignore per the legacy-creep gate. Dead frontend code: ReportPanel/SaveModal/TweaksPanel/HandoffModal (0 importers), OverridePanel (unmounted), mock report/evidence/costs exports, shareableUrl.js building deleted /audit/cold-read routes, FounderDashboard links to nonexistent /report/:id. Orphans: hiveandco/ CF Pages demo outside CI/deploy, _archive/, feature/live-console + fix/inference-target-ssot branches, ec2-drift-snapshot-20260605. Doc drift: AGENTS.md describes routes deleted 2026-05-29, DOSSIER.md marks built services as "build pending" and claims VECTOR-routed inference, gap-query mismatches (ip_reputation orphaned; network_perimeter/waf dead queries). Acceptance: every finding has a recorded decision; docs stop contradicting code.

Lock the VECTOR kill + finish the design-system master. (1) CI guard that fails the proof360 build if VECTOR_URL or localhost:3003 reappears in code/config — the resurrection pattern must not recur a 21st time. (2) Migrate the ~30 chat-family components off inline hex onto the tokens.js master CSS vars so the design template is enforced, not just available.

Extend the proof360 Bedrock-direct pattern across the estate: audit every repo for an unused central /ethikslabs/anthropic/api-key (or per-repo ANTHROPIC_API_KEY) now that Bedrock-direct via the EC2 instance role is the default, and drop it where nothing reads it. Doctrine: no ANTHROPIC_API_KEY when inference is Bedrock.

DONE 2026-06-29: killed 2 empty stray PM2 god daemons + removed dead pm2-ec2-user.service; live /etc/.pm2 now sole daemon; deploy scripts already pinned PM2_HOME=/etc/.pm2; wired+corrected pm2-root.service for reboot resurrection. proof360/argus/corpus-search online + health 200.

Refactor (not warn-suppress) the react-compiler lint violations surfaced by the eslint-plugin-react-hooks v7 bump: react-hooks/set-state-in-effect (OperationalField, MachineDrawer, ThinkingStream), react-hooks/refs (TweaksPanel accessing ref.current in render), react-hooks/immutability. These were downgraded to 'warn' in frontend/eslint.config.js (2026-07-01) to green CI; this task does the real fix so they can go back to error. Restructure the offending effects/refs per react.dev guidance.

Mirror the markerly cast pattern into proof360: seed 2-3 fictional demo customers with real credential logins (shared demo-tier password, admin seat above them) so John/May/partners can hand out logons to walk the customer side and watch the backend accumulate. Markerly reference: MARKERLY/app/scripts/seed-cast.ts (idempotent on completed coverage, admin password from SSM, cast shares the demo password). John approved 2026-07-03 ('will do that with proof360 too').

Make the front door's trust signals REAL and visible (John directive 2026-07-03: 'the subtle logos, sign ons, protections, the little hints mean this is not shopify' — surface the enterprise stack). Slice 1: (a) real Cloudflare Turnstile — create widget for proof360.au, inject sitekey at build (same SSM pattern as Auth0), secret to SSM, server-side siteverify; kill the hardcoded always-pass test key (1x000...AA fallback in Chat.jsx). (b) FounderAuth login: 'Protected by Okta' hint + CF/AWS provenance hints — every hint LIVE-BACKED, never decorative. (c) survey where Vanta trust-report badge/link can surface (free Vanta = real compliance signal). Guardrail: a badge that is not live-backed is the ghost-tenant disease as marketing — estate's edge is that its hints are checkable.

Bind the Turnstile verdict to the login path itself (Codex P2 on PR #11, parked per CODEX-REVIEW-PREMERGE-STANDARD-001). Today the server-side siteverify arms the modal buttons, but a bot can skip the modal and drive the OAuth URL directly — /portal/callback mints founder_auth from any successful redirect. Fix is server-side by necessity: /api/v1/turnstile/verify issues a short-lived signed clearance; enforce it at the first authenticated API attach (journey/profile first-touch), NOT client-side — any sessionStorage/localStorage check is forgeable theater, the exact disease DOOR-001 kills. Scope with slice 1b so the clearance shape serves the Okta/stack hints too.

[P1] scripts/deploy.sh (manual path) hardening — edge-hunt findings #4/#5/#15. (a) Health-check curl races pm2 restart: connection-refused (exit 7) under set -e false-reds a green deploy — wrap in a retry loop with || true (also present in deploy.yml ec2 script). (b) deploy.sh never writes PG_* to api/.env, so a manual deploy ships a founder-memory surface that hits a ghost localhost Postgres and 500s (no global error handler) — add require_ssm PG_HOST/PORT/DATABASE/USER/PASSWORD like deploy.yml does. (c) require_ssm on ANTHROPIC_API_KEY hard-fails on a key the Bedrock-direct runtime never reads — drop it. #4 was previously flagged 'awaiting John ack'; hunt independently confirmed.
tasks.resolution: deploy.sh + deploy.yml hardened: health-check retry loop (non-fatal under set -e, 10x2s), PG_* + PG_MEMORY_DATABASE written from SSM (all 5 /proof360/postgres/* paths verified present), ANTHROPIC_API_KEY require dropped. bash -n + YAML valid. On main.

tasks.boundary: [P2] scripts/deploy.sh (manual path) does not run the DB + memory migrations that deploy.yml runs (run-migrations.js, run-memory-migrations.js). After DEPLOY-HARDEN-001 gave deploy.sh real PG creds, a manual deploy can now reach Postgres but against a schema it never migrates — founder-memory / v3 handlers can 500 on missing tables. Mirror deploy.yml: run both migration scripts (non-fatal, || echo skipped) after writing .env and before the pm2 restart. Also reconcile the remaining deploy.sh vs deploy.yml .env divergence (TRUST360_URL, DASHBOARD_API_URL, PROOF360_ADMIN_KEY, SES_*, REPORT_BASE_URL, DEMO_FOUNDER_MODE) — DEMO_FOUNDER_MODE ties to D3 (kill demo-founder prod posture).

[P2] Founder file-kernel concurrency — edge-hunt #6/#7. memory-store-file.js: concurrent first-touch mints duplicate profiles and loses the loser's transactions (no atomic create-or-get); and a stale lock directory permanently wedges a profile's append path (no lock TTL/steal). Add an atomic profile create + a lock with staleness recovery. Pairs with the DB-side atomicity fix (PR #13).

[P2] Config-drift — edge-hunt #9. VITE_GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID and VITE_MS_CLIENT_ID are baked by neither build path, so partner-portal social login is permanently dead in prod (buttons render, redirect is broken). Either bake them from SSM (same pattern as Auth0/Turnstile) or hide the buttons when unset — do not ship a dead control.

[P2] Login CSRF / session fixation — edge-hunt #11. OAuth `state` is a static constant ('auth0'/'google'), not a per-request anti-CSRF nonce, in Chat.jsx + Portal.jsx. Generate a random state, store it, and verify it in /portal/callback before accepting the code/token. Ties to DOOR-002 (bind the login path).

[P2] Forgeable share link — edge-hunt #12. frontend/src/utils/shareable-url.js builds an unsigned, forgeable cold-read URL AND targets a route deleted in the 2026-05-29 legacy strip (/audit/cold-read). Either sign the params server-side or remove the dead surface.

[P2] Awaited-URL reply silently dropped — edge-hunt #10 (single-verdict, spend-limited verification). Chat.jsx: when a cold-read session already exists, an awaited-URL reply is dropped and awaitingField never clears — founder stuck. Re-verify against the D2 website-reply path (may overlap PROOF360-CER-WEBSITE-REPLY-001, now closed) before fixing.

[P3] CER state-machine hardening — edge-hunt #16/#17/#18 (single-verdict, spend-limited). (a) cer-projection folds by wall-clock ts not append sequence — non-monotonic clock yields wrong status; (b) confirmCer persists + grants consent without asserting the route was confirmed; (c) admin status endpoint allows transitions on a consent-withdrawn CER. Re-verify each against the append-only log semantics before fixing.

[P3] Frontend URL/injection hardening — edge-hunt #19/#20 (single-verdict, spend-limited). Extracted URL reaches the server scrape pipeline with no scheme/host validation (client-side SSRF entry — server SSRF guard from PR #12 is the real defense, but validate scheme client-side too); BrowserPanel renders arbitrary user URLs in an iframe with no sandbable attribute. Add scheme allowlist + iframe sandbox.
